3.happening: We start with an empty stack — it will hold opening brackets that are still waiting to be closed.
3.why: A stack hands back the most recently opened bracket in O(1), which is exactly what a closer needs to check.
3.invariant: The stack holds, bottom to top, every opener seen so far that has not yet been matched.

4.happening: We build a lookup from each closing bracket to the opening bracket it must match.
4.why: An O(1) lookup lets us check any closer against its required opener instantly.
4.invariant: pairs[closer] is the one opener that legally closes that closer.

5.happening: We read the next character '{c}' of the string.
5.why: Validity is decided by scanning left to right and reacting to each bracket in order.
5.invariant: Every character before '{c}' has already been fully processed.

6.happening: We check whether '{c}' is a closing bracket — closers are the keys of the pairs map.
6.why: Openers and closers are handled differently: openers are stored, closers must be matched.
6.invariant: Each character is either an opener (to push) or a closer (to match).

7.happening: '{c}' is a closer, so we check whether the stack is empty.
7.why: A closing bracket with no opener waiting on the stack can never be valid.
7.invariant: If the stack is empty here, there is nothing for '{c}' to close.

8.happening: The stack was empty, so this closer has no partner — the string is invalid.
8.why: A closer with nothing to close breaks the rule that every closer has a matching opener.
8.invariant: result is now False and is about to be returned.

9.happening: We return False immediately — there is no need to scan the rest of the string.
9.why: A single unmatched closer is enough to make the whole string invalid.
9.invariant: The function exits with the verdict False.

10.happening: We pop the top of the stack — the most recently opened bracket — to match it against '{c}'.
10.why: Brackets must close in last-opened-first-closed (LIFO) order, so the top is the only legal partner.
10.invariant: After the pop, top holds the opener that '{c}' is expected to close.

11.happening: We compare the popped opener '{top}' with the opener that '{c}' requires ('{pairs[c]}').
11.why: They must be the same type, otherwise the brackets are crossed or mismatched.
11.invariant: A match keeps the string potentially valid; a mismatch ends it.

12.happening: The popped opener does not match '{c}', so the brackets are mismatched — the string is invalid.
12.why: Open brackets must be closed by the same type, in the correct order.
12.invariant: result is now False and is about to be returned.

13.why: A single mismatch makes the entire string invalid.
13.invariant: The function exits with the verdict False.

15.happening: '{c}' is an opening bracket, so we push it onto the stack.
15.why: It has to be matched later by a closer of the same type, so we remember it.
15.invariant: The top of the stack is always the most recently opened, still-unmatched bracket.

16.happening: The whole string has been scanned; the answer is True only if the stack is now empty.
16.why: Any openers still on the stack were never closed, which makes the string invalid.
16.invariant: An empty stack here means every opener found its matching closer.

17.why: The string is valid exactly when no opener was left unmatched on the stack.
17.invariant: The function exits with True (valid) or False (invalid).

init.happening: We set up the empty stack and the closer-to-opener lookup map.
init.why: The stack tracks unmatched openers; the map lets us check any closer in O(1).
init.invariant: Nothing has been scanned yet — the stack is empty.

loop.happening: We scan the next character of the string.
loop.why: Validity is decided character by character, left to right.
loop.invariant: Everything to the left of the current character is already processed.

check.happening: We decide how to handle the current bracket and, for a closer, test it against the stack's top.
check.why: Openers and closers follow different rules, and a closer must match the most recent opener.
check.invariant: The stack's top is the only bracket a closer is allowed to match.

update.happening: We update the stack — pushing an opener, or popping the top to match a closer.
update.why: The stack must always reflect the openers that are still waiting to be closed.
update.invariant: The top of the stack is the most recently opened, unmatched bracket.

return.happening: We produce the validity verdict for the string.
return.why: The string is valid only if every opener was matched and none are left on the stack.
return.invariant: An empty stack with no mismatch seen means the string is valid.
